The venue regularly hosts a wide array of local and regional talent, ranging from punk and hardcore to indie and burlesque, while its kitchen serves acclaimed Caribbean fusion and Cajun-inspired fare VisitLex, Yelp

attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Ashland, The Henry Clay Estate
attractions.description: Ashland, the historic 19th-century estate of statesman Henry Clay, is a National Historic Landmark located in the heart of Lexington. The 17-acre estate features an Italianate-style mansion, formal gardens, and preserved outbuildings that showcase the legacy of the "Great Compromiser" and the complex history of those who lived and worked there. (VisitLEX, The Henry Clay Estate)
attractions.hours: Mansion, Store & Exhibits: Tue–Sat 10:00 am – 4:00 pm, Sun 1:00 pm – 4:00 pm (Seasonal); Grounds & Gardens: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm daily (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (April – November), Tuesday – Saturday (March and December), Closed Mondays and January–February (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adult: $25, Students (6-college): $15, Children (under 5): Free, Grounds & Gardens: Free (Official Website)
highlights: ['19th-century Italianate mansion filled with authentic artifacts and furnishings', 'Formal English parterre garden maintained by the Lexington Garden Club', '"Traces: Slavery at Ashland" guided tour exploring the lives of the enslaved community', 'Historic outbuildings including an ice house, smokehouse, and carriage house', '17 acres of wooded grounds featuring 300-year-old oak trees and walking trails', "Museum store located in the historic Keeper's Cottage"]
attractions.tips: It is highly recommended to purchase tour tickets online in advance due to limited space. The grounds and gardens are free to visit and provide a peaceful setting for walks, while spring is particularly notable for the blooming Ashland Peonies. (Official Website, Yelp)
attractions.nearby: Chevy Chase shopping and dining district, University of Kentucky, The Arboretum (State Botanical Garden of Kentucky) (VisitLEX)

attractions.name: Ashland: The Henry Clay Estate
attractions.description: Ashland is the historic 17-acre estate and home of Henry Clay, a prominent 19th-century American statesman known as the "Great Compromiser." The site features a meticulously preserved mansion, an accredited arboretum with over 600 trees, and formal gardens, offering a deep dive into Kentucky's political and cultural history. Official About Page
attractions.region: Ashland Park Historic District / Chevy Chase Ashland Park Neighborhood Association, Keeneland Magazine
attractions.hours: Mansion, Exhibits, and Store: Tuesday – Saturday 10:00 am – 4:00 pm, Sunday 1:00 pm – 4:00 pm (Sunday closed in March/December). Grounds & Gardens: 8:00 am – 5:00 pm daily. Hours Page
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (Closed Mondays; closed Sundays in March and December; closed entirely in January and February) Official Website
attractions.admission: $25 Adult, $15 Student (ages 6-college), Free for children under 5 years. A $10 downloadable audio tour is also available for the city context. Tours Page
highlights: 'Guided tours of the historic mansion containing original Clay family belongings. [Tours Page', 'Level 1 Arboretum featuring over 600 trees across 44 species. Grounds Page', 'Formal gardens maintained by the Garden Club of Lexington, including a rare Saunders hybrid peony collection. Grounds Page', 'Historic outbuildings including an 1817 Smokehouse and twin brick icehouses. Grounds Page', "Museum Store located in the Keeper's Cottage offering unique gifts and local items. Store Page", 'Educational exhibits focusing on the lives of all inhabitants, including the women of Ashland and those enslaved on the estate. Learn Page']
attractions.tips: Purchasing tickets online in advance is highly recommended due to limited space. Allow at least one to two hours to explore the mansion, gardens, and walking trails. The first floor of the mansion is wheelchair accessible via a ramp at the back. Visitor Info
attractions.nearby: University of Kentucky, Rupp Arena, Mary Todd Lincoln House, Kroger Field, Chevy Chase neighborhood shops and restaurants. Expedia, VisitLex

attractions.name: Barrel House Distilling Co.
attractions.description: Barrel House Distilling Co. is a small-batch craft distillery located in one of the original barreling houses of the historic Old Pepper Distillery. Formed in 2008 by longtime friends Pete Wright and Jeff Wiseman, it continues the tradition of Kentucky distillers by producing fine spirits like RockCastle Bourbon and Pure Blue Vodka using traditional methods. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 11:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Friday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Tour & Tasting: $15 per person for adults (21+). Active Military and Veterans: $12 per person. Guests 20 & under: Free (cannot participate in tasting). Tastings only: $15 per person. Source
highlights: 'Housed in the historic 18th-century barreling house of the Old Oscar Pepper Distillery. [Wikipedia', 'Produces award-winning spirits including RockCastle Bourbon, Pure Blue Vodka, and Devil John Moonshine. Official Website', 'Features a lounge and taproom called The Elkhorn Tavern, offering craft cocktails and local fare. Official Website', 'Part of the Kentucky Bourbon Trail Craft Tour. Kentucky Bourbon Trail', 'Offers private barrel picks in half or full barrels for a personalized experience. Official Website']
attractions.tips: Tours start on the hour with the final tour beginning at 5:00 PM daily. Plan to spend about an hour for the distillery experience and extra time to browse the unique gift shop or enjoy a cocktail at the adjacent Elkhorn Tavern. Official Website
attractions.nearby: James E. Pepper Distillery, Ethereal Brewing, Goodfellas Pizzeria, The Burl Arcade, Crank & Boom Craft Ice Cream. VisitLEX, Reddit

attractions.name: Beaumont Centre
attractions.description: Beaumont Centre is a prominent mixed-use development and neighborhood in southwestern Lexington, Kentucky, featuring a blend of residential subdivisions, professional office spaces, and retail centers. It serves as a community hub with a public library, an outdoor amphitheater, a YMCA, and extensive walking trails. (Wikipedia, Haymaker Development)
attractions.hours: Area: Open 24/7; Library: Mon-Thu 9:30am-7:00pm, Fri 9:30am-6:00pm, Sat 9:30am-5:00pm, Sun 1:00pm-5:00pm; Kroger: Daily 6:00am-10:00pm (Lexington Public Library, Kroger)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 'Moondance at Midnight Pass Amphitheater for live music and events ([City of Lexington)', 'Beaumont Branch of the Lexington Public Library with Frank Lloyd Wright-inspired architecture (Lexington Public Library)', 'Over 2.5 miles of walking trails connecting residential and commercial areas (Haymaker Development)', 'CM Gatton Beaumont YMCA featuring fitness and community programs (YMCA)', 'Diverse dining and shopping options including Kroger and local cafes (Kroger, Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Visit during the summer for the free 'Summer Nights in Suburbia' concert series at Moondance Amphitheater. The area is highly walkable, allowing visitors to easily navigate between the library, shops, and trails. Parking is plentiful but can be limited during major community events. (City of Lexington, Raya Rivera)
attractions.nearby: Keeneland Race Course, Blue Grass Airport, Fayette Mall, Harrods Hill neighborhood (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Blue Grass Airport Official Website
attractions.description: Blue Grass Airport is a public airport located in Fayette County, Kentucky, approximately six miles west of downtown Lexington. Serving as the primary aerial gateway for central and eastern Kentucky, it is uniquely situated directly across from the world-famous Keeneland Race Course. The airport provides a scenic arrival experience, surrounded by the rolling hills and picturesque horse farms of the Bluegrass region. Wikipedia, Kupi.com Guide
attractions.address: 4000 Terminal Drive, Suite 206, Lexington, KY 40510 Official Website, Allegiant Air
attractions.region: Fayette County / Bluegrass Region Wikipedia, YouTube Guide
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Continuous Operation). TSA Security Checkpoint: Daily 4:00 am - 8:30 pm. Specific shop and restaurant hours vary (e.g., Dunkin' is open daily from 4:15 am - 6:00 pm). Fltplan.com, Official Website, Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (The on-site Aviation Museum of Kentucky has separate admission fees: $18.00 for Adults, $16.00 for Seniors/Veterans, $12.00 for Youth ages 4-17, and Free for children 3 and under) Aviation Museum of Kentucky
highlights: 'Located directly across from the historic Keeneland Race Course, offering unique views of horse country. [Wikipedia', 'Home to the Aviation Museum of Kentucky, featuring a large collection of historic and military aircraft. Aviation Museum of Kentucky', 'Offers non-stop flights to 19 major destinations, including Atlanta, Chicago, and New York. Official Website', 'Features regional shopping and dining options such as The Bourbon Library and Cork & Barrel. Official Website', "Convenient amenities including a Mothers' Nursing Suite and a pet relief area. Official Website", 'Efficient terminal layout allowing for faster check-in and security screening compared to major hubs. Kupi.com Guide']
attractions.tips: Arrive 90 minutes prior to your flight to ensure ample time for parking and security. For plane spotters, the cell phone lot and the airport's three-story parking garage offer excellent views of aircraft takeoffs and landings. If you visit during the horse racing seasons in April and October, you may spot rare private aircraft visiting from around the world. Official Website, YouTube Guide
attractions.nearby: Keeneland Race Course, Aviation Museum of Kentucky, The Kentucky Castle, Downtown Lexington. Wikipedia, YouTube Guide

attractions.name: Blue Stallion Brewing Company
attractions.description: Founded in 2013, Blue Stallion Brewing Company specializes in crafting boldly traditional German-style ales and lagers in the heart of Lexington, Kentucky. The brewery offers a unique hybrid of authentic European brews and modern stylish trends like IPAs and sours, providing a warm and relaxing taproom environment for both enthusiasts and newcomers. (Blue Stallion Brewing)

attractions.admission: Free (Blue Stallion Brewing)

attractions.tips: The taproom is dog-friendly, though owners are asked to take pets outside if they become disruptive. Check the schedule for Liter Specials and weekly events like Test Batch Tuesday for experimental brews. (Bluegrass Bourbon and Eats, Adventures & Ales)

attractions.name: Buster's Billiards & Backroom
attractions.description: Buster's Billiards & Backroom was a premier mid-sized music venue and pool hall located in the historic Lexington Distillery District. Housed in the former Old Tarr Distillery warehouse, the space featured a large state-of-the-art concert hall with a 2,100-person capacity and a separate billiards lounge for a more relaxed atmosphere Buster's B&B Project. It served as a cultural hub for regional and national musical acts until its closure in 2014 Manchester Music Hall.

attractions.admission: Cover charges typically ranged from $5 to $25 depending on the performer Lexington Herald Leader, with some events being free Lexington Herald Leader. Weekly passes for festivals were approximately $25 Lexington Herald Leader.
highlights: 'State-of-the-art concert hall ("Backroom") with a 2,100-person capacity [Buster\'s B&B Project', 'Historic location in the former Old Tarr Distillery warehouse Manchester Music Hall', 'Six professional pool tables with free play on Wednesdays Yelp', "Host to a wide variety of national, regional, and local musical acts Buster's B&B Project", "A major fixture in the revitalization of Lexington's Distillery District MeetingHand"]
attractions.tips: Billiards were famously free on Wednesdays Yelp. Always check the official concert schedule as show times and cover charges varied significantly Lexington Herald Leader. Although now closed, its legacy continues as the Manchester Music Hall Manchester Music Hall.

attractions.name: Calumet Farm
attractions.description: Established in 1924, Calumet Farm is a historic 762-acre Thoroughbred breeding and training facility renowned as the most successful operation in American racing history. Recognizable by its iconic white four-board fences and red-trimmed stables, the farm has produced a record-setting ten Kentucky Derby winners and two Triple Crown champions. Wikipedia, Calumet Farm Official Site
attractions.hours: Not open to the public; Appointments for breeding only. Calumet Farm Official Site
attractions.days_open: Not open to the public. Calumet Farm Official Site
attractions.admission: Not open to the public for tours at this time (Private/Appointments only for breeding/viewing stallions). Calumet Farm Official Site
highlights: 'Record holder for the most Kentucky Derby winners produced (10 winners). [Wikipedia', 'Home to two Triple Crown winners: Whirlaway (1941) and Citation (1948). Calumet Farm Official Site', 'Instantly recognizable by its signature white fences and "devil red" and blue trimmed barns. SAH Archipedia', 'Inducted 11 horses into the National Museum of Racing and Hall of Fame. Wikipedia', 'Leading breeder and owner of Preakness Stakes winners with seven each. Wikipedia', 'Added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1991. Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: While not open for public tours, the farm's iconic white fences and barns can be viewed from Versailles Road and New Circle Road. Many commercial tours, such as Horse Farm Tours Inc., include a "drive-by" of Calumet with historical commentary. Horse Farm Tours Inc., SAH Archipedia
attractions.nearby: Keeneland Race Course, Blue Grass Airport, Kentucky Horse Park, Aviation Museum of Kentucky. KAYAK, VisitLEX

attractions.name: Central Bank Center (formerly Lexington Center)
attractions.description: The Central Bank Center is a premier entertainment, convention, and sports complex in downtown Lexington, featuring over 200,000 square feet of flexible meeting space and the iconic Rupp Arena. Recently expanded through a $310 million transformation, it serves as the vibrant heart of the city's urban gathering spaces and a regional hub for major events, trade shows, and University of Kentucky basketball (Official Website, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Main Office: Monday - Friday 8:30 am - 5:30 pm; Ticket Office (Walk-Up): Wednesday & Friday 12:00 pm - 4:00 pm; Event hours vary (Official Website, Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Events daily; Office: Monday - Friday) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Admission varies by event; Free to enter the complex, but tickets are required for arena, opera house, and most convention events (Official Website)
highlights: "Home to the 20,545-seat Rupp Arena, the famous venue for University of Kentucky Men's Basketball ([Official Website)", 'Includes the historic 1,000-seat Lexington Opera House, a premier performing arts center (Official Website)', 'Offers 100,000 square feet of state-of-the-art exhibition space and a 24,300 square foot ballroom (VisitLex)', 'Adjacent to the beautiful Triangle Park, which features waterfalls and seasonal ice skating (Wikipedia)', 'Connected to the Hyatt Regency Lexington for convenient lodging and event access (Wikipedia)', 'LEED Certified facility following a major $310 million architectural expansion (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Adhere to the strict clear bag policy (max 12"x12"x6") for all arena events; cashless payments are required for parking; arrive early for large events as downtown parking can be limited; and check the event calendar for specific entry times which typically start 60-90 minutes before showtime (Official Website, Official Website).

The complex features over 200,000 square feet of flexible meeting space, including state-of-the-art exhibit halls, ballrooms, and the historic 1,000-seat Lexington Opera House. It also serves as the home to the iconic Rupp Arena and is an anchor for the city's vibrant urban gathering spaces. Central Bank Center, VisitLex

attractions.name: Cheapside Historic District (Henry A. Tandy Centennial Park) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Cheapside Historic District is a significant public square in downtown Lexington that dates back to 1780. Historically one of the South's largest slave markets, the area has been reclaimed as a vibrant community hub and was renamed Henry A. Tandy Centennial Park in 2020 to honor a prominent African American stone mason and community leader. (Wikipedia, Lexington Herald-Leader)
attractions.hours: Daily: Dawn to Dusk; Lexington Farmers Market: Saturdays 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM (Dec-Mar) or 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Apr-Nov); Thursday Night Live: Thursdays 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM (seasonal) (VisitLEX, Town & Tourist)
attractions.admission: Free (VisitLEX)
highlights: 'Weekly Lexington Farmers Market held year-round on Saturdays ([VisitLEX)', 'Thursday Night Live summer concert series with live music and food trucks (VisitLEX)', 'Home to the historic Fifth Third Bank Pavilion and the Old Fayette County Courthouse (MKSK Studios)', 'Renamed in 2020 to honor Henry A. Tandy, a formerly enslaved person who became a successful entrepreneur and mason (Lexington Herald-Leader)', "Historically served as the 'Public Square' and a major marketplace since the city's founding (Wikipedia)", "Features historical markers detailing the site's role in the domestic slave trade and the city's early history (Kentucky Historical Society)"]
attractions.tips: Arrive early on Saturday mornings for the best selection at the Lexington Farmers Market, as it is a popular local tradition. During the summer, Thursday Night Live is a great way to experience local music and culture, but it can get crowded, so consider walking or using downtown parking garages. Take time to read the historical markers around the square to understand the complex and sobering history of the site. (Town & Tourist, My Old Kentucky Road Trip)
attractions.nearby: Old Fayette County Courthouse, Mary Todd Lincoln House, Lexington Opera House, Gratz Park, 21c Museum Hotel (VisitLEX)

attractions.name: Chevy Chase Historic District (also known as Chevy Chase Neighborhood) (VisitLEX, Chevy Chase Neighborhood Association)
attractions.description: Chevy Chase is a historic neighborhood in Lexington, Kentucky, founded in the 1920s by Henry Clay Simpson on land once part of the Ashland estate. This charming area is less than one square mile and is celebrated for its tree-lined streets, bungalows, and a vibrant village-like atmosphere filled with local boutiques and diverse dining options. It serves as a walkable bridge between the University of Kentucky and downtown Lexington, maintaining a strong sense of community and architectural character (VisitLEX, Homes.com).
attractions.address: Centered around the intersection of East High Street and Euclid Avenue, Lexington, KY 40502 (VisitLEX)
attractions.region: Chevy Chase / Ashland Park area (Apple Maps)
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Shops and Restaurants: Typically Monday-Saturday 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sunday hours vary (VisitLEX, Yelp)
attractions.admission: Free (VisitLEX)
highlights: "Chevy Chase Inn (CCI), Lexington's oldest continuously operating bar dating back to 1933 ([VisitLEX)", "A vibrant walkable commercial district featuring unique boutiques like AJ's and Peggy's (VisitLEX)", "Diverse local dining favorites such as Josie's, Bourbon n’ Toulouse, and Bear & The Butcher (VisitLEX)", 'Historic residential architecture including 1920s bungalows, Tudor cottages, and Colonial Revival homes (Scorsone Group)', 'The annual Chevy Chase Street Fair held every August, which closes down the 800 block of Euclid Avenue (VisitLEX)']
attractions.tips: Chevy Chase is extremely walkable; visitors should plan to spend a day exploring the shops and eateries on foot. Parking is conveniently available at Chevy Chase Plaza, and it is highly recommended to visit during the annual street fair in August for a full community experience (VisitLEX).
attractions.nearby: Ashland (The Henry Clay Estate), University of Kentucky, Ecton Park, and Downtown Lexington (VisitLEX, Apple Maps)

attractions.name: Coldstream Regional Park
attractions.description: Coldstream Regional Park is a expansive 224-acre public park featuring scenic green spaces, a popular 10-acre fenced dog park, and a significant section of the 12-mile Legacy Trail. Formerly a prominent Kentucky horse farm, it now serves as a major recreational hub with paved and dirt trails for walking, cycling, and jogging.
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (typically 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['10-acre fully fenced dog park with separate areas for large and small dogs', 'Direct access to a 1.8-mile section of the 12-mile paved Legacy Trail', 'Lush green spaces and open fields for picnics, walks, and outdoor meetings', 'Host site for various community events and races, including 5K runs', 'Hydration stations featuring water fountains with dog-friendly bowls', 'Scenic views of Cane Run creek and historical ties to the McGrathiana Stud horse farm']
attractions.tips: The park has limited shade, so visitors are encouraged to bring hats and plenty of water for themselves and their pets on hot days. The dog park requires all dogs to be licensed and vaccinated, and owners must supervise their pets at all times.
attractions.nearby: Kentucky Horse Park, Embassy Suites by Hilton Lexington/UK Coldstream, Marriott Griffin Gate Golf Resort and Spa, Lexmark Corporate Headquarters, University of Kentucky Main Campus

attractions.name: Comedy Off Broadway
attractions.description: Comedy Off Broadway is Lexington's premier destination for stand-up comedy, established in 1987. The venue hosts a mix of nationally recognized headliners and rising local talent in an intimate, cabaret-style setting. Comedy Off Broadway, VisitLex

attractions.name: Darby Dan Farm (Darby Dan Farm)
attractions.description: Darby Dan Farm is a historic, full-service Thoroughbred breeding and stallion operation that has made a unique transition from a private legendary estate to a commercial powerhouse. Rooted in distinguished private history, the farm is committed to the excellence of equine athletes from the yearling paddock to the racetrack or sales ring (Darby Dan Farm).

attractions.region: North Lexington / Old Frankfort Pike corridor (VisitLex)

attractions.name: Headley-Whitney Museum of Art
attractions.description: The Headley-Whitney Museum of Art is a decorative arts museum founded in 1968 by jewelry designer George Headley and his wife Barbara Whitney. Located in the heart of the Kentucky Bluegrass, it features a diverse permanent collection of fine and decorative arts, including jewels, bibelots, and unique architectural structures like a Shell Grotto and a Jewel Room designed as a jewelry box (Headley-Whitney Museum, Wikipedia).

attractions.tips: Parking is free and located in the lot through the main entrance. The museum is closed during the winter months. Visitors can also honor pets with engraved bricks in the pet walkway around the grounds (Headley-Whitney Museum, My Scenic Drives).

attractions.name: Hunt-Morgan House (Hopemont)

The house serves as a museum interpreting the lifestyle of 19th-century Kentucky affluence and was the home of Confederate General John Hunt Morgan and Nobel laureate Dr. Thomas Hunt Morgan (Blue Grass Trust, American Battlefield Trust).

Founded in 1936, it is celebrated for its commitment to the traditions of the sport, its stunning limestone architecture, and its beautifully landscaped grounds. (Keeneland About, Wikipedia)

The facility features a variety of house-made beers and ciders, a dog-friendly patio, and a community-focused atmosphere with frequent events like trivia and yoga. It was a staple of the Lexington craft scene for eight years before announcing its closure in late 2024. Lexington Herald-Leader, Bluegrass Bourbon and Eats, Edible Kentucky

attractions.name: Raven Run Nature Sanctuary
attractions.description: Raven Run Nature Sanctuary is a 734-acre preserve dedicated to protecting the natural beauty of the Kentucky River Palisades. It features over 10 miles of hiking trails through forests, meadows, and creeks, along with significant historic and geological landmarks. (VisitLex, Raven Run official site)
attractions.hours: May - September: 8am - 7pm (Gate closes 6pm); April: 8am - 6pm (Gate closes 5pm); November - March: 9am - 5pm (Gate closes 4pm). (Raven Run official site)
attractions.days_open: Open daily except for Thanksgiving and December 24-26 (Raven Run FAQ)
attractions.admission: Free (VisitLex, Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Kentucky River Overlook: A 70-foot bluff providing scenic views of the Kentucky River Palisades. ([Raven Run Trails)', 'Historic Evans Mill: The site of a grist mill built in 1833. (Raven Run Trails)', 'Prather Homestead: Ruins and history from the family that owned the land in the early 1800s. (Raven Run Trails)', 'Freedom and Discovery Trails: Paved, barrier-free trails for accessibility. (Raven Run Trails)', 'Nature Center: Offers educational resources, wildlife exhibits, and small concessions. (Raven Run official site)']
attractions.tips: Pets and bicycles are not allowed to protect the wildlife and local ecosystem. Visitors should check the official site for reservation requirements during high-visitation periods. (Raven Run FAQ, Lexington Parks)

attractions.name: Red Mile Gaming & Racing
attractions.description: Established in 1875, Red Mile is the second oldest harness racing track in the world and the oldest horse racing venue in Lexington (Wikipedia). The facility features a iconic one-mile track made of red clay and was re-imagined in 2015 as a premier entertainment destination combining historic racing with over 900 gaming terminals (Red Mile Racing).
attractions.hours: Gaming & Facility: Monday - Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 AM; Wednesday 10:00 AM through Monday 4:00 AM (24-Hour Weekends) (Red Mile Gaming). Live Racing: Sunday - Tuesday at 1:00 PM during the season, typically July through October (Red Mile Racing, Red Mile Racing).
attractions.admission: Free admission for live racing and general entry to the facility (Red Mile Racing, Red Mile Gaming). Note: Must be 21+ to enter the gaming floor (Airial Travel).
highlights: 'Second oldest harness track in the world, founded in 1875 ([Red Mile Racing).', "Home to the Kentucky Futurity, one of the three jewels in Trotting's Triple Crown (Red Mile Racing).", 'Distinctive one-mile track surface made of local red clay (Wikipedia).', 'Over 900 Historical Horse Racing (HHR) gaming terminals with 138 different themes (Red Mile Racing).', "On-site Caesars Sportsbook and multiple dining options like Frankie's Bar & Grill (Red Mile Gaming).", 'Hosts major events like the Railbird Music Festival in the infield (Wikipedia).']
attractions.tips: Visitors can enjoy free soft drinks in the casino area while playing (Airial Travel). Be aware that some parking is on grass and can become muddy during wet weather (Airial Travel). Check the live racing calendar in advance as the meet is seasonal (Red Mile Racing).
attractions.nearby: Keeneland Race Course, University of Kentucky, Kentucky Horse Park, Rupp Arena, Mary Todd Lincoln House (Wikipedia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Rupp Arena at Central Bank Center (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Located in the heart of downtown Lexington, Rupp Arena is a premier multi-purpose arena recognized as an epicenter of sports and entertainment excellence. It serves as the historic home court for the University of Kentucky Men’s Basketball program and regularly hosts world-class concert tours, family shows, and major sporting events like the "Sweet 16" high school tournament. (Central Bank Center)

attractions.hours: Box Office Walk-Up: Wed & Fri 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM; Customer Service Phone: Tue–Fri 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Event hours vary by show. (Central Bank Center)
attractions.days_open: Varies by event schedule; Box Office is open Wednesday and Friday. (Central Bank Center)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typical prices for sporting events range from $5 (youth/senior general admission) to $12+ (premium reserved), while children 5 and under are often free for general admission. (UK Athletics)
highlights: "Home of the legendary University of Kentucky Wildcats men's basketball team ([Wikipedia)", 'One of the largest indoor basketball arenas in the United States with a capacity of 20,500 (Central Bank Center)', 'Named after iconic Kentucky basketball coach Adolph Rupp (Wikipedia)', 'Part of the larger Central Bank Center convention and shopping complex (Central Bank Center)', 'Features the "eRUPPtion Zone," a dedicated standing-room area for passionate students (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 90 minutes early for major events to clear security; purchase tickets through the official box office or Ticketmaster to avoid excessive third-party fees; and utilize the nearby LexPark garages or the Rupp Arena lot for convenient parking. (Central Bank Center, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill (Official Site)
attractions.description: Shaker Village of Pleasant Hill is a National Historic Landmark that preserves the legacy of the Kentucky Shakers across 3,000 acres of rolling Bluegrass countryside. It features 34 restored 19th-century buildings, offering a window into a communal lifestyle through immersive exhibits, farm-to-table dining, and working farm demonstrations. Visitors can explore more than 36 miles of trails and discover the craftsmanship and spirituality that defined the Pleasant Hill community between 1805 and 1910 (Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Exhibits & Tours: Tue-Sun 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Closed Monday). Hiking Trails: Mon-Sun 6:00 AM – 8:30 PM (or dusk-dawn if earlier). The Trustees' Table (Dining): Sun-Sat Breakfast 7:30–10:00 AM, Lunch 11:30 AM – 3:00 PM, Dinner 5:00 – 8:30 PM (Official Site).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday for exhibits and tours; hiking trails are open daily (Official Site).
attractions.admission: Ages 13-61: $20, Ages 6-12: $10, Seniors (62+)/Military/First Responders: $15, Ages 5 & Under: Free. Reduced $3 admission for cardholders through Museums for All (Official Site).
highlights: '34 restored original 19th-century Shaker buildings ([Official Site)', "Famous twin spiral staircases inside the 1839 Trustees' Office (National Park Service)", '3,000-acre landscape featuring a working farm and nature preserve (Official Site)', 'Over 36 miles of hiking, multi-use, and equestrian trails (Maple Hill Manor)', "Farm-to-table dining at the award-winning Trustees' Table (Official Site)", 'Seasonal riverboat excursions aboard the Dixie Belle (VisitLex)']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for the expansive grounds and gravel paths. Reservations are strongly suggested for all meals at The Trustees' Table. Staying overnight at The Inn on property includes complimentary admission to the village (Worthy Detours, Official Site).
attractions.nearby: High Bridge Park (Tripadvisor), Old Fort Harrod State Park (Expedia), Kentucky River Palisades (Maple Hill Manor), Mary Todd Lincoln House (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Short Street District (North Broadway-Short Street Historic District)
attractions.description: The Short Street District is a vibrant and historic area in downtown Lexington known for its beautiful architecture, strings of overhead lights, and concentration of upscale dining and nightlife. Originally a commercial hub in the 19th century, the district now serves as a lively destination for locals and visitors, featuring unique murals, historic landmarks like the Lexington Opera House, and popular restaurants.

attractions.hours: Daily 24 hours (Individual business hours vary; restaurants typically open for lunch/dinner and bars until late night)
attractions.admission: Free (Public district; individual businesses and attractions like the Lexington Opera House have their own pricing)

attractions.tips: Visit on a Saturday morning to experience the nearby Lexington Farmers Market, or on a Thursday evening in the summer for the 'Thursday Night Live' concert series. Parking is free on Saturdays and Sundays in most metered spaces downtown.

attractions.name: South Hill Historic District National Park Service
attractions.description: The South Hill Historic District is a vibrant residential neighborhood south of downtown Lexington, featuring a significant collection of early 19th and 20th-century architecture. Designated as a local historic district in 1972, it is home to the Adam Rankin House, the city's oldest extant residence, and showcases diverse styles including Federal, Greek Revival, and Italianate. National Park Service, Historic South Hill Neighborhood Association

attractions.name: Spendthrift Farm
attractions.description: Spendthrift Farm is a historic 1,200-acre thoroughbred stallion farm and breeding operation in the heart of Kentucky's Horse Country. It is home to legendary champions such as Kentucky Derby winner Authentic and leading sire Into Mischief, offering visitors an immersive look into the world of elite horse breeding. The farm features the B. Wayne Hughes Visitor Center, where guests can explore the rich heritage and legacy of "The Breeders' Farm." Spendthrift Farm Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: The Aviation Museum of Kentucky
attractions.description: The Aviation Museum of Kentucky is a non-profit institution dedicated to preserving the state's aeronautical history and providing aerospace education. Located in a 20,000-square-foot facility at Blue Grass Airport, it features a diverse collection of restored military and civilian aircraft, flight simulators, and the Kentucky Aviation Hall of Fame. Official Website, Wikipedia

attractions.hours: Regular (April-Dec): Tue-Sat 10 AM-5 PM, Sun 1 PM-5 PM; Winter (Jan-Mar): Tue-Fri 11 AM-4 PM, Sat 10 AM-5 PM, Sun 1 PM-5 PM; Closed Mondays Official Website

attractions.name: The Lexington Cemetery
attractions.description: The Lexington Cemetery is a historic 170-acre private, non-profit rural cemetery and world-class arboretum established in 1849. Renowned for its park-like beauty, the grounds serve as a community memorial that documents the social, political, and environmental history of the region. It features a diverse collection of over 200 tree species and is famous for its extensive spring floral displays, including thousands of blooming tulips and dogwoods (Official Website, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Grounds Hours) (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (VisitLex, Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Designated arboretum featuring over 200 species of trees ([Official Website)', 'The 120-foot tall Henry Clay Monument, a National Historic Landmark (Wikipedia)', 'Lexington National Cemetery, containing the remains of over 1,000 Civil War soldiers (NPS)', 'Spectacular spring blooms with 16,000 tulips, crabapples, and magnolias (Official Website)', 'Final resting place of notable figures including Vice President John C. Breckinridge and actor Jim Varney (Wikipedia)', 'Picturesque lakes and curvilinear pathways designed in the rural cemetery tradition (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The best time to visit is during the spring (late March to April) to witness the peak bloom of tulips and dogwoods. Self-guided tour maps are available at the front office to help visitors navigate the expansive grounds and locate historic monuments. Please note that the cemetery is a place of serenity and respect; professional photography requires prior authorization from management (Official Website, Kentucky Historic Travels).

attractions.name: The Lexington Philharmonic
attractions.description: The Lexington Philharmonic (LexPhil) is a premier professional orchestra based in Lexington, Kentucky, dedicated to fostering excellence and innovation in live symphonic music. Founded in 1961, it serves a diverse community across central and eastern Kentucky with a rich season of classical, pops, and educational programming. Under the leadership of Music Director Mélisse Brunet, LexPhil continues to bring distinction to the region through transformative musical experiences (The Lexington Philharmonic).

attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday, 9:00 am - 5:00 pm; ArtsPlace Office: Tuesday & Thursday, 9:00 am - 5:00 pm (Know Before You Go)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office); Scheduled Performance Dates (Know Before You Go)
attractions.admission: Tickets range from $30 to $80; Youth (17 and under) and students: $11; Some community events are Free (The Lexington Philharmonic, Know Before You Go)

attractions.tips: Tickets can be purchased online, by phone, or in person at the ArtsPlace building. Arrive early as doors typically open 30-45 minutes before performances, and late seating is at the discretion of the House Manager. Student and youth discounts are available for most shows with a valid ID (Know Before You Go).

attractions.name: The Square (formerly Victorian Square) (The Square)
attractions.description: The Square is a historic landmark in downtown Lexington consisting of sixteen late-Victorian and early 20th-century commercial buildings that were integrated into a single 140,000-square-foot complex in 1985. Listed on the National Register of Historic Buildings, it serves as a vibrant urban hub featuring upscale retail stores, art galleries, restaurants, and the Lexington Children's Museum. (The Square, Lane Report)
attractions.hours: Shops: Mon-Sat 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM, Sun 12:00 PM - 6:00 PM; Restaurants: Daily 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM (later on Fri-Sat) (Urban Outfitters, Tony's, Pies & Pints)
attractions.admission: Free to enter; individual attractions such as the Explorium of Lexington charge admission (e.g., $10 for adults/children) (Lexington Children's Museum, VisitLex)
highlights: 'Preserved historical architecture including decorative metal ceilings and ironwork ([The Square)', "Home to the Artists' Attic, a collection of studios and an exhibition gallery (The Square)", "Features the Explorium of Lexington (Lexington Children's Museum) (Lexington Children's Museum)", "Houses top-tier dining like Tony's Steaks & Seafood and Pies & Pints (Tony's, Pies & Pints)", 'Integral part of the downtown pedway system connecting to hotels and Rupp Arena (Wikivoyage))']
attractions.tips: Park at the Victorian Square Garage on Short Street for the most direct access; explore the top floor to see local artists at work in the Artists' Attic; use the pedway system to reach the complex during inclement weather (LexPark, Wikivoyage))

attractions.name: Thoroughbred Park Triangle Foundation
attractions.description: Thoroughbred Park is a 2.75-acre tribute to the horse industry, featuring thirteen life-size bronze sculptures that depict the spirit of Kentucky racing. Visitors can walk among seven racehorses rushing toward a finish line or observe broodmares and foals in a tranquil "meadow" setting. The park also serves as an outdoor museum, with dozens of plaques honoring the men and women who have shaped the history of Thoroughbred racing Triangle Foundation, VisitLex.

highlights: 'Thirteen life-size bronze sculptures created by artist Gwen Reardon [Triangle Foundation', 'Seven racehorses and jockeys captured in a dramatic "Track" finish Triangle Foundation', 'A dedicated bronze statue of the legendary 19th-century stallion, Lexington Triangle Foundation', '44 plaques honoring influential figures in the Thoroughbred breeding and racing industry Triangle Foundation', 'Landscaped areas and water features symbolic of the Triple Crown of Thoroughbred Racing Triangle Foundation']

attractions.description: Known as the 'Niagara of the South,' Cumberland Falls State Resort Park features a 125-foot wide curtain of water falling into a boulder-strewn gorge in the Daniel Boone National Forest. It is famous as one of the few places in the world to regularly produce a 'moonbow,' a lunar rainbow visible during full moon nights.

attractions.tips: To see the moonbow, visit during a clear night within two days before or after a full moon and head to the main observation decks via the short Cumberland Falls Trail. Wear sturdy shoes for hiking as many trails like Eagle Falls can be rugged and challenging. Note that the DuPont Lodge and Riverview Restaurant are undergoing renovations in 2026, so check the official site for current facility availability.

attractions.description: Lake Cumberland is one of the largest and most popular lakes in the United States, spanning over 60,000 acres of water and 1,225 miles of shoreline. The State Resort Park serves as a premier destination for houseboating, fishing, and outdoor recreation in the rolling hills of Southern Kentucky. Kentucky State Parks, Lake Cumberland Tourism
attractions.region: Russell County / Jamestown Kentucky State Parks
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Park Grounds); Lodge/Cabin check-in 4:00 PM; Pool: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Seasonal). Facebook, Kentucky State Parks
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week) Kentucky State Parks
attractions.admission: Free to enter the park. Pool daily fee: 12 and under $5.00, 13 and older $7.00. Kentucky State Parks
highlights: 'Houseboating capital of the world with the largest collection of rental houseboats. [Lake Cumberland Vacation', 'Over 50,000 acres for fishing largemouth, smallmouth, and Kentucky bass. Kentucky State Parks', 'Miles of scenic hiking trails through beech, oak, and hickory forests. Kentucky State Parks', 'Wolf Creek National Fish Hatchery and visitor center located nearby. Cabins on the Cumberland', 'Full-service marina with boat rentals, fuel, and lakeside dining. Timber Pointe Resort', '18-hole miniature golf course and disc golf course on-site. Kentucky State Parks']
attractions.tips: Plan ahead and book boat rentals and lodging early for summer weekends. Fuel up and stock up on supplies in Somerset or Jamestown before heading out on the water. Bring sunscreen and bug spray for the humid summer months. Lake Cumberland Tourism
attractions.nearby: Wolf Creek National Fish Hatchery, Cumberland Falls State Resort Park, Mill Springs Battlefield National Monument, Somerset (Somernites Cruise), and Mini-Indy Go-Karts. Cabins on the Cumberland, Timber Pointe Resort

attractions.name: Natural Bridge State Resort Park
attractions.description: Natural Bridge State Resort Park is a scenic public recreation area featuring a massive 65-foot-high, 78-foot-long natural sandstone arch. Surrounded by the Daniel Boone National Forest, the park offers 22 miles of hiking trails, a 60-acre lake, and a dedicated nature preserve protecting rare species. Kentucky State Parks, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Open 24/7 (Trails recommended dawn to dusk). Sky Lift: 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM daily (Seasonal: mid-March to late October). Pool: Sat-Sun 11:00 AM - 7:00 PM for the public (Memorial Day through Labor Day) Kentucky State Parks, Natural Bridge Sky Lift
attractions.days_open: Daily (some facilities like Sky Lift and Pool are seasonal) Kentucky State Parks
attractions.admission: Free for park entry and parking. Sky Lift: approximately $15 for adults (round trip); Pool: $6 for adults (13+), $5 for children (12 and under) for non-lodge guests Kentucky State Parks, Hiking With Shawn
highlights: ['The 78-foot long Natural Bridge sandstone arch centerpiece', "22 miles of scenic hiking trails including the 'Original Trail'", 'Seasonal Sky Lift offering ridge-top views of the surrounding forest', "Unique rock formations like Balanced Rock and 'Fat Man's Squeeze'", 'Mill Creek Lake for fishing, canoeing, and kayaking', 'Historic Nature Center and diverse birdwatching opportunities']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking boots for the rugged terrain and steep inclines. Take the Sky Lift for an easier ascent if you want to skip the hike to the top, then walk across the bridge for stunning views. Visit in October for the best fall foliage or May for birdwatching. Kentucky State Parks, Red River Gorge Tourism
attractions.nearby: Red River Gorge Geological Area, Daniel Boone National Forest, Miguel's Pizza, Gladie Visitor Center Kentucky State Parks, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Red River Gorge Geological Area
attractions.description: The Red River Gorge is a uniquely scenic canyon system within the Daniel Boone National Forest, designated as a National Geological Area and National Natural Landmark. It features spectacular rock formations, including over 100 natural sandstone arches and towering cliffs, making it a premier destination for hiking and world-class rock climbing.
attractions.region: Daniel Boone National Forest (Cumberland Ranger District)
attractions.hours: Area: 24/7; Gladie Visitor Center: Thursday through Monday 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
attractions.admission: Free (Backcountry camping permit required for overnight use: $5 per vehicle/day, $7 per 3 days, $50 annual)
highlights: ['Over 100 natural sandstone arches, the densest concentration east of the Rockies.', "Home to the 900-foot Nada Tunnel, often called the 'Gateway to Red River Gorge.'", 'World-renowned rock climbing destination with over 1,500 bolted sport and traditional routes.', '19.4 miles of the Red River are designated as a National Wild and Scenic River.', 'Rich archaeological history with 664 known sites dating back 11,000 years.', 'Diverse ecosystem with unique flora like the white-haired goldenrod, found only in this gorge.']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking boots as trails are rugged and can be slippery; the Nada Tunnel is one-lane, so check for oncoming headlights before entering; back-country camping is permitted but requires a pass displayed in your vehicle; stay on marked trails to protect the fragile ecosystem and avoid venomous snakes.
attractions.nearby: Natural Bridge State Resort Park, Nada Tunnel, Kentucky Reptile Zoo, Miguel's Pizza, Muir Valley Nature Preserve

attractions.name: Three Chimneys Farm (Three Chimneys Farm)
attractions.description: Three Chimneys Farm is a premier Thoroughbred breeding operation established in 1972. It is renowned for housing legendary stallions like Seattle Slew and current stars such as Gun Runner, maintaining a legacy of excellence in the American horse racing industry (Three Chimneys Farm, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 1981 Old Frankfort Pike, Versailles, KY 40383 (Three Chimneys Farm)
attractions.hours: Tours are by appointment (often at 1:00 PM); Farm office hours are Mon-Fri 7:30am-4:00pm, Sat-Sun 7:30am-12:00pm (Three Chimneys Farm, My Scenic Drives)
attractions.days_open: Tours are available by appointment, typically Monday through Friday from April to October (VisitLex, My Scenic Drives)
attractions.admission: Nominal fee of $10 for guided tours (often donated to charity); Open House events are free (TripAdvisor, Three Chimneys Farm)
highlights: 'Home to 2017 Horse of the Year and champion sire Gun Runner ([Three Chimneys Farm)', 'Former residence of legendary Triple Crown winner Seattle Slew (Wikipedia)', 'Located along the scenic and historic Old Frankfort Pike (VisitLex)', 'Offers behind-the-scenes tours of world-class stallion complexes and breeding facilities (VisitLex)', 'Features statues and a cemetery honoring Thoroughbred legends (YouTube)']
attractions.tips: Tours should be booked well in advance through Horse Country or directly with the farm, especially during peak months like April and October. Visitors are advised to wear comfortable walking shoes and be mindful of the rules of a working stallion farm (TripAdvisor, VisitLex PDF).
attractions.nearby: WinStar Farm, Keeneland Race Course, The Kentucky Castle, Headley-Whitney Museum, Equus Run Vineyard & Winery (TripAdvisor)

attractions.name: Wild Turkey Distillery Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Perched high above the Kentucky River in Lawrenceburg, the Wild Turkey Distillery is a living symbol of Kentucky’s deep-rooted bourbon heritage Bourbon Barrel Cottages. The site features the modernized Jimmy Russell Wild Turkey Experience visitor center, which reflects over a century of bourbon-making tradition through immersive tours and tastings PR Newswire.
attractions.region: Wild Turkey Hill Wild Turkey Official Website
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Production Tour: From $25; Premium Bourbon Tasting: From $45; Born from the Barrel - Russell’s Reserve Immersion: From $65; Russell’s Reserve Connoisseur Whiskey Tasting: From $45 Bourbon Barrel Cottages
highlights: 'Jimmy Russell Wild Turkey Experience: A state-of-the-art 12,000 square foot visitor center [PR Newswire', 'Generations Lounge: Offers signature cocktails and breathtaking views of the Kentucky River Kentucky Bourbon Trail', 'Production Tour: A guided walk-through of the fermentation, distillation, and barrel-filling areas VisitLex', 'Historic Rickhouses: Explore the massive seven-story timbered warehouses where bourbon ages Bourbon Barrel Cottages', 'Russell Family Legacy: Potential to meet legendary Master Distiller Jimmy Russell in person Tripadvisor', 'Premium Tastings: Guided flights of high-end expressions like Russell’s Reserve and Rare Breed Wild Turkey Official Website']
attractions.tips: Tours and tastings are highly sought after and should be booked online 2-6 months in advance Alcohol Professor. Visitors should plan to spend at least 90 minutes on-site to fully enjoy the tour and visitor center amenities Kentucky Bourbon Trail. The outdoor pavilion offers some of the best panoramic views of the Kentucky River valley in the region PR Newswire.
attractions.nearby: Four Roses Distillery, Woodford Reserve Distillery, Whiskey Thief Distilling Co., Lovers Leap Winery, Rising Sons Home Farm Winery, and the Bluegrass Scenic Railroad and Museum Tripadvisor, Whisky Advocate
